entities.name: Tatian
entities.description: An author or figure cited regarding the nature of the devil's body.

attributes.claim: affirms the devil's body consists of spiritual congealments of fire and air

entities.name: Aristotle
entities.description: A philosopher cited regarding the effects of melancholic passions.

attributes.claim: states that melancholic moods can lead to certain behaviors in persons subject to it

entities.name: Ade Davie
entities.description: A Kentish woman who believed she had bargained her soul to the devil due to a melancholic episode.

entities.name: Ant. Houin
entities.description: A witch executed in 1565 who confessed to causing weather phenomena.

entities.name: melancholie
entities.description: A medical/psychological condition described as causing false imaginations, delusions, and irrational behaviors.

effects: deprives or depraves judgment and senses
can cause people to imagine they are monarchs, beasts, or earthen pots

entities.name: the devil's covenant/bargain
entities.description: The alleged agreement between witches and the devil, which Scot argues is unproven and often based on false confessions.

attributes.nature: alleged league/fellowship; described by some as involving physical contact with the devil

entities.name: transubstantiation
entities.description: An impossible claim made in witches' confessions, used by Scot to argue against the validity of proofs.

summary: In this passage from Reginald Scot's 'Discoverie of Witchcraft', the author argues against the validity of witchcraft_ref
